content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow Daily Update (Apr 28, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on April 28, 2026, focused on expanding the plugin's architectural capabilities. Three new pull requests were opened to introduce advanced routing, runtime context management, and credential lifecycle handling.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*   **Routing and Context Management**
    *   Introduced `TriggerContext` to facilitate originating-conversation routing ([#26](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/26)).
    *   Added `RuntimeContextProvider` extension point to improve runtime data handling ([#25](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/25)).
*   **Security and Credential Lifecycle**
    *   Implemented `delete(userId, credType)` functionality to support disconnect-purge operations ([#24](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/24)).
    *   Hardened rules for credentials, runtime-facts, and output fields ([#25](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/25)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#26](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/26): feat(types,service): add TriggerContext for originating-conversation routing.
*   [#25](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/25): feat(prompt,types): add RuntimeContextProvider extension point + harden credentials/runtime-facts/output-field rules.
*   [#24](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/pull/24): feat(credential-store): add delete(userId, credType) for disconnect-purge.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None.

## NEW ISSUES
*   None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   [#79](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-n8n-workflow/issues/79) Dependency Dashboard: Recent activity indicates that a stack of PRs is open against the `develop` branch to address dependency updates. Progress is currently gated by the requirement to merge [#7146](https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza/pull/7146) first, as `bun install` is currently broken on `develop` due to a missing transitive dependency. Contributors noted that CI workflows for code quality require updates to properly invoke `bun install` and `tsc` to prevent future regressions.
